card_description: Lord Running Clam is a divine or mythological figure referenced in Philip K. Dick's Exegesis. Appears in 2 Exegesis passages. Context: "Dick identifies his internal "guide" as St."

linked_segments.summary: Dick identifies his internal "guide" as St. Luke, "the beloved physician," who is a non-terrestrial entity of high authority. He reinterprets the ending of *Tears* as a ciphered "Acts of the Apostles"
linked_segments.title: U_Claudia_36.txt

linked_segments.summary: Dick refines the "Black Iron Prison" metaphor, describing a metal prison breached by "live signals" (roots) that teach slaves how to lift the siege. He identifies "James-James" (the Creator) as a pote
linked_segments.title: U_Claudia_37.txt
